queue.task: Study Volcano planner SELECT * + window bug — what would it take to fix?
queue.summary: SELECT * + window bug: planner skips early projection when hasWindowFns=true (line 584), then final projection at line 725 uses star: true which expands to window-internal columns. Fix: at final projection, expand * to base table columns only, then append window result columns. Moderate complexity — need to track base-table schema through Window operator. Not a rewrite but needs careful column tracking. Est: 1-2 BUILD tasks.

queue.task: Audit AdamW implementation: verify weight decay, bias correction, epsilon placement
queue.summary: AdamW audit: moments, bias correction, epsilon placement, and weight decay decoupling all correct per Loshchilov & Hutter 2019. BUT found bug: this.step increments per-parameter-update instead of per-optimizer-step. With N params, param 1 gets step=1, param 2 gets step=2 etc. — bias correction diverges between params. Fix: increment step once per optimizer step, not per update() call.

queue.task: Read buffer-pool.js: is the LRU eviction policy correct? What happens under memory pressure?
queue.summary: Buffer pool is well-implemented: fixed frames + free list, pin counting, WAL force before dirty eviction, LRU/Clock replacer. 23/23 tests pass. Under memory pressure (all pinned): returns null — correct but no backpressure/retry. Good quality code.

queue.task: Study LoRA implementation: are rank-1 updates mathematically correct?
queue.summary: LoRA implementation is mathematically correct per Hu et al. 2021. Verified: A/B decomposition, zero-B init, α/r scaling, forward (base + LoRA path), backward (dB, dA, dInput), merge, frozen base. Numerical gradient check: 9.2e-13 (machine precision). Clean code.

queue.task: Numerical gradient check: attention backward (SelfAttention) for correctness
queue.summary: SelfAttention backward verified: numerical gradient check 1.45e-13 (machine precision). Correct chain rule through Wo, softmax, Q/K/V projections and biases. 7/7 gradient checks all pass now.

queue.task: Study B-tree implementation: is insert/delete/search correct? Edge cases?
queue.summary: B+ tree: 56/56 tests pass (unit, depth, stress). Insert with split, range scan via linked leaves, suffix trees for non-unique keys all correct. One simplification: no merge/rebalance on delete (tree can become unbalanced). Acceptable for prototype.

queue.task: Audit PPO implementation: is the clipped surrogate objective correct?
queue.summary: PPO audit: clipped surrogate objective correct (min with negation for loss). GAE correct (reverse accumulation with gamma-lambda). RLHF reward formula correct. approxKL uses exact r-1-log(r) formula. Minor: klPenalty computes reverse KL direction (common approximation, works in practice). 8/8 tests pass.

queue.task: Study cost model: how are selectivity estimates computed? Are they reasonable?
queue.summary: Cost model is reasonable PostgreSQL-style: equality uses 1/ndistinct, range uses min/max, IN uses nValues/ndistinct. Join cardinality at 10% (crude but functional). Sort O(nlogn), NL join O(nm) correct. Missing: histograms, MCV, correlation. Good for a prototype.

queue.task: Study quantization.js: does it correctly simulate INT8 quantization?
queue.summary: Quantization correct: symmetric INT8 absmax and per-channel both verified. 5.3x compression, 52-56 dB SNR, max error ~0.001. Edge cases handled (zero rows). Missing: asymmetric/zero-point quantization, INT4, calibration. Good for post-training quantization.

queue.task: Read expression-evaluator.js: are SQL expression types handled correctly?
queue.summary: Expression evaluator: 1191 lines, 113 case statements. Handles SQL NULL correctly (== null covers null+undefined, arithmetic with null returns null, division by zero returns null). Supports: arithmetic, comparisons, CAST, aggregates, boolean logic, IN/EXISTS/LIKE, CASE WHEN. Thorough implementation.

queue.task: Audit DPO implementation against Rafailov et al. 2023 paper
queue.summary: DPO implementation textbook-correct per Rafailov et al. 2023. Verified: loss formula, log-ratio over response tokens only, numerically stable log-sigmoid, implicit reward β·log(π/π_ref), gradient multiplier σ(-diff). 6/6 tests pass. High quality code with proper numerical stability.

queue.task: Study the parser: what SQL syntax is supported? What is missing?
queue.summary: Parser is 3206 lines — largest file in project. Hand-written recursive descent. Supports: CTEs (recursive), UNION/INTERSECT/EXCEPT, window fns with frames, MERGE, RETURNING, CASE WHEN, subqueries, all comparison operators, IN/LIKE/BETWEEN/EXISTS, all join types. Missing: LATERAL, GROUPING SETS, PIVOT, JSON operators. Very comprehensive for a hand-written parser.

queue.task: Study Volcano iterators: verify open/next/close protocol across all 15+ iterator types
queue.summary: 19 Volcano iterator types all follow open/next/close protocol. 72 tests pass (3+30+39). queue.task: Study reward-model.js: is the Bradley-Terry preference model correct?
queue.summary: Reward model: Bradley-Terry loss correct (-log σ(r_w - r_l)), numerically stable. BUT bias gradients (db1, db2) are never computed — biases stay at 0 forever. Weights learn fine but biases are dead. 6/6 tests pass despite this (biases not needed for tests). Fix: add bias gradient accumulation in trainStep.

queue.task: Investigate the AFTER DELETE trigger bug from constraint-crash-depth.test.js
queue.summary: Found root cause of AFTER DELETE trigger bug! _fireTriggers wrapper has 5 params but callers pass 6 args. The 6th arg (oldRowValues for OLD.column) is silently dropped by JS. OLD.column references are never substituted in trigger body. Fix: add 6th param to wrapper: _fireTriggers(timing, event, table, row, schema, oldRow).

queue.task: Audit learning-rate schedule: cosine annealing with warmup — correct formula?
queue.summary: LR schedule correct: linear warmup (step+1/warmupSteps), cosine decay (minLr + 0.5(maxLr-minLr)(1+cos(π·progress))), boundary conditions verified. WSD schedule also correct. Presets match published GPT-3/Llama-2 configs.

queue.task: Study the stats collector: does ANALYZE produce useful statistics?
queue.summary: Stats collector: comprehensive — row count, NDV, null fraction, min/max, MCV (top-k), equi-height histograms. Selectivity estimation uses MCV lookup with 1/NDV fallback. Supports sampling. Has features the cost model doesnt use yet (MCV, histograms). Good foundation for smarter query planning.

queue.task: Study tokenizer-pipeline.js: does BPE merging work correctly?
queue.summary: BPE tokenizer: correct implementation of Sennrich et al. 2016. Encode/decode roundtrip preserves text. 19/19 tests pass (12 BPE + 7 pipeline). Iterative pair counting + merging works correctly. Tokenizer pipeline adds pre/post processing.

queue.task: Audit pruning.js: magnitude pruning — is mask application correct?
queue.summary: Pruning: magnitude, structured, lottery ticket, gradual schedule all implemented. Minor inconsistency: >= vs > threshold between Matrix/Array paths. Lottery ticket matches Frankle & Carlin 2019. Cubic pruning schedule matches Zhu & Gupta 2017. 3/3 structured pruning tests pass. No dedicated magnitude pruning test file.

queue.task: Audit RoPE: verify frequency precomputation and rotation application
queue.summary: RoPE correct: θ_i = 1/10000^(2i/d), rotation via [cos,-sin;sin,cos], backward via transpose (orthogonal matrix). Numerical gradient check: 1.77e-12. Position offset for KV cache. Confirmed: gqa-attention.js import bug is just applyRoPEToSequence → should be applyRoPE.

queue.task: Study checkpoint-handler.js: is WAL checkpointing correct?
queue.summary: Checkpoint: simple marker-based approach. WAL writeCheckpoint syncs to disk, findLastCheckpoint locates replay boundary. getRecordsAfterCheckpoint provides records for recovery. Simplified ARIES approach — no dirty page table or active transaction list in checkpoint. Adequate for prototype.

queue.task: Audit KV cache: is the sliding window / eviction correct?
queue.summary: Two KV cache impls: per-head (kv-cache.js) and per-layer (kv-cache-impl.js). Both correct: pre-allocated, append-only, O(1) per new token. Both throw on overflow — no sliding window or eviction. Clone support for beam search. Missing: sliding window (Mistral-style), ring buffer, paged attention.

queue.task: Study matview-handler.js: does materialized view refresh work?
queue.summary: Matview: full-refresh implementation. CREATE executes query + stores in real table. REFRESH truncates + re-inserts. Cache invalidation on refresh. Correct but no incremental refresh. Schema inference simplistic (number=INT, else=TEXT).

queue.task: Audit cross-attention.js: encoder-decoder attention backward
queue.summary: Cross-attention forward correct: Q from decoder, K/V from encoder, scaled dot-product, multi-head, numerically stable softmax. Attention pooling also correct. No backward pass — forward-only. 6/6 tests pass.

queue.task: Study procedure-handler.js: does stored procedure execution work?
queue.summary: Procedure handler: text-based parameter substitution with proper string escaping. SQL functions execute as SELECT, return first col/row. TABLE return type supported. JS functions via new Function() (eval-like, security concern). Limitation: param names in string literals get substituted.

queue.task: Study gradient-accumulator.js: does micro-batch accumulation work?
queue.summary: Gradient accumulator: correct. Sums gradients over accumSteps micro-batches, averages on completion. Reset zeroes buffer. Ready signal for optimizer coordination. Standard implementation — simulates large batches without extra memory.

queue.task: Audit SwiGLU: is the gating mechanism correct per Shazeer 2020?
queue.summary: SwiGLU correct per Shazeer 2020. Forward: (xW1)⊙swish(xWgate)→W2. Backward: all 6 weight+bias gradients + dInput. Numerical gradient check: 1.66e-13 (machine precision). 9th module verified.

queue.task: Study heap-file: how are pages managed? Is compaction correct?
queue.summary: Heap file: FSM tracks free space, insert reuses space, update does in-place or delete+insert. Record IDs are stable (pageId, slotId). No explicit compaction — fragmentation handled by FSM reuse. Vacuum handler may compact. Clean 125-line implementation.

queue.task: Study mixed-precision training: does it correctly simulate FP16 forward + FP32 backward?
queue.summary: Mixed precision: correct FP16 simulation (range, underflow, 10-bit mantissa), BF16 (7-bit), dynamic loss scaling (grow/backoff), overflow detection + skip, master FP32 weights. Matches Micikevicius et al. 2018 and PyTorch GradScaler behavior.

queue.task: Audit row-lock.js: does row-level locking prevent phantom reads?
queue.summary: Row locks: FOR UPDATE/SHARE, NOWAIT, SKIP LOCKED all correct. Lock compatibility matrix right (SHARE+SHARE OK, UPDATE conflicts). Does NOT prevent phantom reads (would need predicate locking). Expected limitation — even PostgreSQL needs SERIALIZABLE for phantom prevention.

queue.task: Study speculative decoding: is the accept/reject logic correct?
queue.summary: Speculative decoding correct per Leviathan/Chen 2023. Accept prob = min(1, p_target/p_draft). Reject → sample from max(0, p_target-p_draft). Break on first rejection (correct — cant accept after reject). All-accepted bonus token. Preserves exact target distribution. Implementation matches the paper.

queue.task: Study join executor: how does join order optimization work?
queue.summary: Join ordering: real System R-style DP over bitmask subsets. Only INNER joins reordered (outer preserved). Tracks join graph for connectivity. ≤6 table limit (O(2^n)). Uses table stats for cost estimation. Genuinely sophisticated for a prototype DB.

queue.task: Run gradient check on Dense layer backward
queue.summary: Dense(ReLU) backward verified: 3.72e-13 gradient error. 10th module to pass numerical gradient check. All core backward passes in the neural-net library are mathematically correct.

queue.task: Audit embedding.js: is token + position embedding correct?
queue.summary: Embedding: correct lookup table with 1/√d init, ID clamping, sparse backward (only updates seen token rows). Output flattened to [batch, seqLen*dim] (2D Matrix limitation). Standard implementation.

queue.task: Study WAL format: how are records serialized? Is the format durable?
queue.summary: WAL format: binary header (20 bytes: length+LSN+type+CRC32) + JSON payload + trailing length for backward scan. 12 record types. 16MB segment rotation. fsync for durability. CRC32 per-record integrity. Well-designed — mirrors PostgreSQL WAL structure.

queue.task: Audit dropout.js: is the inverted dropout scaling correct?
queue.summary: Dropout: correct inverted dropout. Scale 1/(1-p) during training, mask reused in backward, no scaling at inference. Standard implementation per Srivastava 2014 + modern inversion.

queue.task: Deep-read: slotted-page.js — how are records stored on pages?
queue.summary: Slotted page: textbook implementation. Header→slots→free→records layout, forward/backward growth. Lazy delete, in-place update when fits, automatic compaction. Matches Ramakrishnan & Gehrke page organization. Clean 134 lines.

queue.task: Study top-p-sampling.js: nucleus sampling correctness
queue.summary: Nucleus sampling correct per Holtzman 2020. Temperature → softmax (stable) → sort descending → cumulative ≥ p → renormalize → sample. Also top-k and combined top-k+top-p. Clean implementation.

queue.task: Audit LSTM implementation: are forget/input/output gates correct?
queue.summary: No LSTM/RNN/GRU implementation exists in neural-net. Notable gap — these are foundational sequence models. The library jumped from MLPs to transformers. Adding LSTM+GRU would round out the coverage.

queue.task: Study paginated-exec.js: how does cursor-based pagination work?
queue.summary: Paginated exec: simple offset pagination — executes full query then slices. Not true cursor-based (materializes all rows). Fine for small datasets, inefficient for large. Returns metadata (page, totalRows, hasNext/Prev).

queue.task: Study positional-encoding.js: sinusoidal encoding vs RoPE
queue.summary: Sinusoidal PE: correct per Vaswani 2017. sin/cos alternating with frequency decay. Adds to input (absolute position). RoPE: rotates Q/K (relative position). Both correct implementations of different approaches. Also has timestep embedding for diffusion.

queue.task: Audit deserialize-handler.js: does data restoration work correctly?
queue.summary: Deserialize: restores tables, indexes (rebuilt from heap scan), views, triggers, sequences, matviews, comments, index catalog. Correct approach — indexes rebuilt rather than serialized. Minor: hardcoded BPlusTree(32) regardless of original order.

queue.task: Audit GAN: does the adversarial training loop work?
queue.summary: GAN correct per Goodfellow 2014. D training: real→0.9 (label smoothing), fake→0. G training: fake→1 (non-saturating loss). Only G weights updated during G step. Correct discriminator freezing pattern. Full training loop with shuffling.

queue.task: Study savepoint-handler.js: nested savepoint support
queue.summary: Savepoint: full-snapshot approach — clones all rows on SAVEPOINT. Rollback restores heap + rebuilds indexes. RELEASE removes savepoint stack. Nested savepoints via stack. O(total data) per savepoint (expensive but correct). Cache invalidated on rollback.

queue.task: Study weight-init.js: Xavier, He, Kaiming initialization correctness
queue.summary: Weight init correct: Xavier (Glorot 2010) √(6/(fin+fout)), Kaiming (He 2015) √(2/fin), orthogonal via Gram-Schmidt, Box-Muller normal sampling. All match published formulas.

queue.task: Study compiled-query.js: what does query compilation do?
queue.summary: Compiled query engine: JIT compilation for queries with ≥5K rows. Integrates cost-based planner with pipeline codegen. Adaptive threshold (benchmarked: compiled is 3x slower below 1K rows). Falls back to Volcano when compilation fails or isnt worthwhile. 851 lines — sophisticated optimization layer.

queue.task: Git push + daily log update
queue.summary: Git push + daily log updated.

queue.task: Audit loss functions: MSE, cross-entropy, hinge — are gradients correct?
queue.summary: Loss functions: MSE forward correct but gradient missing 1/n factor (forward divides by 2n, gradient is just p-t without /n). Cross-entropy assumes softmax output (gradient = p-t), which is correct for that case. Epsilon clamping for numerical stability.

queue.task: Study pg-server.js: does the PostgreSQL wire protocol work?
queue.summary: pg-server.js: real PostgreSQL wire protocol v3 implementation at 1936 lines. Handles: MD5 auth, query execution, RowDescription+DataRow messages, type OID mapping, parameter status. Can connect with psql! Impressive for a prototype database.

queue.task: Study VAE implementation: is the reparameterization trick correct?
queue.summary: VAE correct per Kingma & Welling 2014. Reparameterization: z = μ + exp(0.5·logvar)·ε (makes sampling differentiable). KL divergence: closed form for Gaussian. β-VAE support (Higgins 2017). Encode → latent sample → decode pipeline correct.

queue.task: Read planner.js: how does the query planner choose between scan types?
queue.summary: Query planner: cost-based access path selection. TABLE_SCAN vs INDEX_SCAN based on IO+CPU cost. B+tree height estimated via log100(N). DP join reordering for multi-table. 890 lines. Correctly prefers index for low selectivity, scan for high.

queue.task: Audit relative-position-bias.js: is T5-style relative attention correct?
queue.summary: T5 relative position bias correct per Raffel 2020. Bucketing: exact for small distances, logarithmic for large. Bidirectional (separate pos/neg). Learnable bias table (numBuckets × nHeads). Matches T5/HuggingFace implementation.

queue.task: Study result-cache.js: how does query result caching work?
queue.summary: Query cache: SQL+params key, FIFO eviction (maxSize=1000), table-based invalidation via string matching. extractTables parses FROM/JOIN/INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE. Hit rate tracking. Simple but functional — FIFO could be upgraded to LRU, string-match invalidation could over-invalidate.

queue.task: Run gradient check on GELU activation backward
queue.summary: GELU exists in activation-functions.js but NOT in activation.js (which Dense uses). Two disconnected activation modules: activation.js (Dense-compatible with backward: sigmoid, relu, tanh, softmax) and activation-functions.js (standalone: gelu, silu, mish, elu, selu). Dense layers cant use GELU — design inconsistency.

queue.task: Audit index-advisor.js: does it recommend useful indexes?
queue.summary: Index advisor: workload-based recommendation engine at 437 lines. Analyzes query patterns (WHERE, JOIN, ORDER BY, GROUP BY). Tracks column access frequency, identifies composite candidates, checks existing indexes. Generates CREATE INDEX SQL. Inspired by pg_qualstats. Sophisticated for a prototype.

queue.summary: Git push done.

queue.task: Audit diffusion.js: is the forward diffusion + denoising process correct?
queue.summary: DDPM correct per Ho et al. 2020. Forward: x_t = √ᾱ·x_0 + √(1-ᾱ)·ε. Linear + cosine (Nichol 2021) schedules. Model predicts noise, MSE loss. Cumulative alpha product for closed-form any-step sampling.

queue.task: Study pipeline-compiler.js: what does the JIT codegen produce?
queue.summary: Pipeline compiler: push-based compilation inspired by HyPer (Neumann 2011). Identifies pipeline segments between breakers (Sort, HashAggregate, etc). Generates tight JS functions eliminating Volcano virtual dispatch. Same approach as HyPer/DuckDB. 342 lines.

queue.task: Audit contrastive-learning.js: is SimCLR/InfoNCE loss correct?
queue.summary: SimCLR/NT-Xent correct per Chen et al. 2020. Cosine similarity + temperature scaling + numerically stable log-softmax. Self-exclusion in denominator. Positive pair accuracy metric. Matches paper formulation exactly.

queue.task: Study transactional-db.js: how does it extend the base Database?
queue.summary: TransactionalDatabase: full ACID layer at 1404 lines. Integrates DiskManager, BufferPool, FileBackedHeap, FileWAL, MVCC/SSI. Session-based transactions. Catalog persistence. WAL recovery on open. Snapshot and serializable isolation. This is the full database engine.

queue.task: Study text-generation.js: autoregressive decoding correctness
queue.summary: Text generation: greedy decode (argmax per step) + repetition detection (window-based pattern matching). Simple but correct for autoregressive generation.

queue.task: Audit partition.js if exists: table partitioning
queue.summary: Table partitioning: range (boundary-based) and hash (DJB2). Partition pruning skips irrelevant partitions on range predicates. Insert routes to correct partition. Both strategies well-implemented.

queue.task: Audit knowledge-distillation.js: is the soft-label loss correct?
queue.summary: Knowledge distillation correct per Hinton et al. 2015. Temperature-scaled softmax, KL(teacher||student) direction correct, T² compensation factor, α-weighted combination with hard cross-entropy loss. Standard formulation.

queue.task: Git push + daily log
queue.summary: Git push done.

queue.task: Audit MoE (mixture of experts): is the gating mechanism correct?
queue.summary: MoE correct per Shazeer 2017. Router (linear→scores), top-k routing with softmax over selected, weighted expert combination, load balancing auxiliary loss. No capacity factor (simplification). Each expert is independent FFN.

queue.task: Study vector.js: what vector operations are supported?
queue.summary: Vector store: pgvector-inspired with cosine, euclidean, dot product KNN. Brute-force linear scan (O(n)). Metadata-filtered search. No approximate index (HNSW/IVF). Clean 100 lines.

queue.task: Study normalization.js: what normalization variants exist?
queue.summary: Normalization module: LayerNorm, RMSNorm, InstanceNorm. All correct. RMSNorm (Zhang & Sennrich 2019) — no mean subtraction, just 1/√(mean(x²)+ε). Used in LLaMA/Mistral. InstanceNorm = LayerNorm for 2D (correct).

queue.task: Audit concurrency: deadlock detection in row-lock.js
queue.summary: No deadlock detection in row-lock.js. Relies on NOWAIT (throw immediately) or SKIP LOCKED (skip locked rows). Since HenryDB is single-threaded, deadlocks are impossible in current architecture. Would need wait-for graph cycle detection for concurrent sessions.

queue.task: Audit optimizer.js: SGD momentum implementation
queue.summary: SGD momentum: correct v=μv+lr·g. Adam in optimizer.js has explicit step() method — better than AdamW (separate file) which auto-increments per-param. Adam also has L2 weight decay (coupled, not decoupled like AdamW). RMSProp presumably correct too. Key states per-param via Map.

queue.task: Study explain.js: EXPLAIN ANALYZE output
queue.summary: EXPLAIN system: PostgreSQL-style output at 874 lines. Plan nodes with estimated costs/rows + actual costs/rows (ANALYZE). Tree-structured output with proper indentation. Multiple formats. Predicate pushdown integration. Very polished feature.

queue.task: Study ResNet skip connections: is the identity shortcut correct?
queue.summary: Residual connection correct per He et al. 2015. Forward: output = input + sublayer(input). Backward: dInput = dOutput + sublayer.backward(dOutput) — gradient flows through both paths (identity skip + sublayer). Clean implementation.

queue.task: Git push + daily log
queue.summary: Git push done.

queue.task: Audit normalizing-flows.js: is the change of variables formula correct?
queue.summary: Normalizing flows: Planar (Rezende 2015) + Affine Coupling (RealNVP, Dinh 2017). Both correct. Planar: log-det Jacobian via ψ trick, invertibility constraint via softplus. Affine coupling: diagonal Jacobian, exact inverse. Change of variables formula correct.

queue.task: Study vectorized.js: columnar execution engine
queue.summary: Vectorized engine: MonetDB/X100 style at 463 lines. Column batches (1024 rows), columnar storage, batch operators. HenryDB now has THREE execution models: Volcano (row-at-a-time), Pipeline compiler (JIT push), Vectorized (columnar batch). Impressive for a prototype.

queue.task: Study adaln.js: is adaptive layer norm for diffusion correct?
queue.summary: AdaLN correct per DiT (Peebles & Xie 2023). Predicts γ,β from conditioning via MLP. AdaLN-Zero: α gate for residual (init 0 → identity). Used in Diffusion Transformers.

queue.task: Study SSI (serializable snapshot isolation): does it detect anomalies?
queue.summary: SSI correct per Cahill 2008 / Ports & Grittner 2012. Tracks rw-antidependencies, detects dangerous structures (inConflict + outConflict on same tx), aborts to prevent anomalies. Same approach as PostgreSQL SERIALIZABLE. 277 lines. Academic-quality implementation.

queue.task: Study char-lm.js: character-level language model
queue.summary: Character LM: 831-line end-to-end GPT-style model. CharTokenizer + Embedding + Decoder Transformer + Causal Attention + AdamW + Cosine LR + Text Generation. 13/13 tests pass including training convergence on repeating patterns. Working integration demo!

queue.task: Audit pushdown.js: predicate pushdown optimization
queue.summary: Predicate pushdown: splits WHERE into conjuncts, identifies table refs, pushes single-table predicates below joins. Classic optimization. 154 lines. Reduces rows before join (early filtering).

queue.task: Study darts.js: differentiable architecture search
queue.summary: DARTS (Liu et al. 2019): continuous relaxation of architecture search. Mixed ops as softmax-weighted sums, alpha params learned via gradient descent. Cell-based architecture. 359 lines. Simplified but core concept is correct. Cutting-edge NAS.

queue.task: Git push + daily log
queue.summary: Git push done.

queue.task: End of day: compile all findings into actionable tomorrow task list
queue.summary: Compiled actionable TODO.md for tomorrow: 9 neural-net quick fixes, 4 HenryDB fixes, 2 nice-to-haves. All bugs from Session B depth exploration organized by priority and estimated effort.

queue.task: Final git push + session end
Final git push + CURRENT.md session end + daily log summary
Audit sampling.js: repetition penalty and temperature sampling
queue.summary: Sampling: temperature, top-k, top-p (nucleus), repetition penalty (Keskar 2019), greedy, combined — all correct. Repetition penalty correctly handles positive (÷penalty) and negative (×penalty) logits. Numerically stable softmax throughout.

queue.task: Study file-backed-heap.js: disk-based heap file
queue.summary: File-backed heap: 369 lines, binary page format via DataView (not JSON). Slotted page on disk with header (pageId, numSlots, freeSpaceEnd, pageLSN). Buffer pool integration. Tuple encoding/decoding. Proper database storage engineering — binary, not serialized.

queue.task: Study microgpt.js: what architecture decisions were made?
queue.summary: MicroGPT: 211-line clean GPT-style model. Embedding → PE → N×TransformerEncoder → LayerNorm → Dense(softmax). Uses existing infrastructure. Second e2e model (alongside char-lm). Key differences from char-lm: uses softmax output (char-lm uses custom), uses existing TransformerEncoderBlock.

queue.task: Study disk-manager.js: page I/O
queue.summary: DiskManager: 191 lines, CMU 15-445 style. Fixed 4KB pages at pageId*pageSize offset. Read/write with fstatSync. Stats tracking. Clean bottom-of-stack implementation.

queue.task: Audit optimizer-zoo.js: what exotic optimizers exist?
queue.summary: Optimizer zoo: SGD Momentum, AdaGrad (history accumulation), RMSprop (exponential decay), Lion (Chen 2023 — sign-based update, decoupled weight decay). All correct. Lion is notably simple — only uses sign of interpolated momentum.

queue.task: Study visibility-map.js: how does MVCC visibility work?
queue.summary: MVCC visibility: exact PostgreSQL model. Snapshot {xmin, xmax, activeSet}. Visibility: <xmin=visible, >=xmax=invisible, between=check activeSet. Version chain walk. Write-write conflict detection. READ COMMITTED (refresh per stmt) and REPEATABLE READ (frozen snapshot). 523 lines. Textbook correct.

queue.task: Git push + daily log
queue.summary: Git push done.

queue.task: Study matrix.js: core tensor operations — is anything slow or incorrect?
queue.summary: Matrix.js: 256 lines, Float64Array storage. Core ops: dot (O(n³), correct but cache-unfriendly access pattern for K dimension), transpose, add/sub/mul, map, sum, argmax, slice. No bugs found. Double precision is why gradient checks achieve 1e-13 accuracy.

queue.task: Study vector-clock.js: distributed system primitive
queue.summary: No vector-clock module. BUT discovered HenryDB has 30+ additional data structures including: ART (adaptive radix tree), B-epsilon tree, bloom filters, bloom join, bitmap index, AVL tree, circular list, band join, advisory locks, audit log. The project is much larger than initially surveyed.

queue.task: Audit lr-scheduler.js: cosine annealing + warm restarts
queue.summary: LR scheduler: Constant, CosineDecay, StepDecay, OneCycle (Smith 2018), WarmupScheduler (composable). All formulas correct. No warm restarts (SGDR/T_mult). Clean composable design.

queue.task: Study vectorized-codegen.js: codegen for vectorized engine
queue.summary: Vectorized codegen: DuckDB-style columnar batch + JIT. FOURTH execution model in HenryDB (Volcano, Pipeline, Vectorized, VecCodegen). 454 lines. Generates tight columnar loops for scans and joins. Impressive breadth of execution strategies.

queue.task: Study maxpool.js: is the backward pass correct for max pooling?
queue.summary: MaxPool2D backward correct: routes gradient only to max-index position (saved during forward). Standard approach. Tie-breaking: first max wins (correct for argmax). No learnable params. Channel-preserving pooling.

queue.task: Study decorrelation.js: subquery decorrelation technique
queue.summary: Decorrelation: 432 lines. Transforms IN/EXISTS subqueries → semi-joins. Detects correlation via column reference analysis. Key optimization: O(n²) nested loop → O(n+m) hash semi-join. 15/15 tests pass (verified earlier).

queue.task: Git push + daily log
queue.summary: Git push done.

queue.task: Study ART (adaptive radix tree): modern index structure
queue.summary: ART (Leis et al. 2013): 4 adaptive node types (4/16/48/256) with proper growth. Node4→16→48→256. 281 lines. Matches original paper. Used in HyPer/DuckDB for in-memory indexes.

queue.task: Study bloom-join.js: bloom filter join optimization
queue.summary: Bloom join: build Bloom filter on join key column, probe to pre-filter non-matching rows. 1% false positive rate. Stats tracking (build/probe time, filtered vs passed rows). 132 lines. Effective for multi-way join pruning.

queue.task: Study b-epsilon-tree.js: write-optimized index
queue.summary: B-epsilon tree: write-optimized B-tree with message buffers (Brodal & Fagerberg 2003). Buffer at internal nodes, flush when full → sequential I/O. Used in TokuDB/BetrFS. 188 lines. Advanced write-optimized index structure.

queue.task: Study conv1d.js: is the 1D convolution correct?
queue.summary: Conv1D: im2col/col2im approach like Conv2D but for sequences. Output length = floor((len+2*pad-kernel)/stride)+1. Kaiming init. Forward, backward, MaxPool1D all implemented. 276 lines.

queue.task: Study bitmap-index.js: bitmap index for OLAP
queue.summary: Bitmap index: Uint32Array bit-vectors per value. AND/OR/NOT via bitwise ops. Popcount for counting. 201 lines. Standard OLAP bitmap index for low-cardinality columns.

queue.task: Audit data-augmentation.js: what transforms exist?
queue.summary: Data augmentation: Gaussian noise, Mixup (Zhang 2018), CutMix (Yun 2019). Beta sampling simplified to uniform (should be proper Beta distribution for Mixup). Basic but functional augmentation pipeline.

queue.task: Study adaptive-engine.js: how does the engine choose execution strategy?
queue.summary: Adaptive engine: cost-based strategy selection with runtime feedback! Analyzes query, picks best engine (Vectorized/Codegen/Compiled/Volcano), tracks execution time per query shape, adapts over time. 307 lines. Genuinely sophisticated — CockroachDB/Oracle-level concept.

queue.task: Git push + daily log
queue.summary: Git push done.

queue.task: Study bloom-filter.js: is the hash function and bit array correct?
queue.summary: Bloom filter: FNV-1a + double hashing (Kirsch & Mitzenmacher 2004). Optimal m/k calculation. Uint32Array bit array. 184 lines. Correct implementation with serialization support.

queue.task: Study advisory-locks.js: application-level locks
queue.summary: Advisory locks: PostgreSQL-compatible. Session + transaction level. Exclusive + shared modes. Wait queue (FIFO). HAS deadlock detection (while row-lock.js does not!). 260 lines. Very complete implementation.

queue.task: Study cardinality.js: cardinality estimation methods
queue.summary: HyperLogLog (Flajolet 2007): register-based with alpha correction, small/large range corrections. FNV-1a hash. clz32 for leading zeros. 100 lines. Also supports merge for distributed use. Correct implementation.

queue.task: Study multi-task-learning.js: shared representations
queue.summary: No multi-task learning module exists. Gap in the library.

queue.task: Study band-join.js: range-based join
queue.summary: Band join: sort-merge-sweep for range predicates (BETWEEN). Correct approach — hash join cant handle ranges. O(nlogn + mlogm + matches). 110 lines. Good for temporal/spatial joins.

queue.task: Study curriculum.js: curriculum learning
queue.summary: No curriculum learning source module (test exists but no src). Gap.

queue.task: Study audit-log.js: change data capture
queue.summary: Audit log: 9 event types (CONNECT, DISCONNECT, AUTH, DDL, DML, QUERY, ERROR, PRIVILEGE_CHECK). Per-entry: session, user, IP, statement, rows, duration. Circular buffer. Query/filter support. 212 lines. Enterprise-level audit capability.

queue.task: Git push + daily log
queue.summary: Git push done.

queue.task: Study access-method.js: pluggable index types?
queue.summary: Access method: 26-line pluggable index interface. insert/lookup/scan/rangeScan. Registry for plugging in new index types. Clean abstraction layer.

queue.task: Study analytics.js: OLAP capabilities
queue.summary: Analytics: T-Digest (Dunning) for streaming quantile estimation + Segment Tree for range queries. T-Digest used by Redis, Elasticsearch, Druid. 177 lines.

queue.task: Study catalog.js: system catalog management
queue.summary: Catalog: 29-line in-memory system catalog. Tables, indexes, column types. Simple CRUD. The real metadata is in db.tables Map — this is the formal catalog layer.

queue.task: Study attention-is-all-you-need.js: Vaswani 2017 implementation
queue.summary: No dedicated attention-is-all-you-need module. The Vaswani 2017 architecture is split across existing modules: attention.js, transformer.js, positional-encoding.js.

queue.task: Study aggregate-cache.js: cached aggregate results
queue.summary: Aggregate cache: TTL + table-level invalidation. SQL normalization for keys. LRU eviction. Dependency tracking (table→cached queries). 236 lines. Solves the dashboard-query problem for read-heavy workloads.

queue.task: Study focal-loss.js: class imbalance handling
queue.summary: Focal loss (Lin 2017): -αt(1-pt)^γ·log(pt). Correct with alpha balancing, gamma modulation. γ=0 → cross-entropy. Also: Dice loss for segmentation (1 - 2|A∩B|/(|A|+|B|)). Hinge loss, Huber loss also in loss-functions.js.

queue.task: Git push + daily log
queue.summary: Git push done.

queue.task: Study plsql.js: PL/SQL language support
queue.summary: PL/HenryDB: full PL/pgSQL-like procedural language at 854 lines! Parser + interpreter. DECLARE/BEGIN/END, IF/ELSIF/ELSE, WHILE, FOR (REVERSE), RETURN, RAISE, EXECUTE (dynamic SQL), EXCEPTION handlers, variable scoping. Complete procedural language interpreter embedded in the database. Incredibly sophisticated.

queue.task: Study query-vm.js: SQL query virtual machine
queue.summary: Query VM: SQLite VDBE-style bytecode engine! 30+ opcodes (control flow, data, arithmetic, comparison, string, aggregate, hash). Register file + bytecode compiler + interpreter. 595 lines. This is the FIFTH execution model in HenryDB! Five distinct engines in one database.

queue.task: Study group-by-executor.js: GROUP BY execution
queue.summary: GROUP BY executor: 528 lines. Handles column names, expressions, aliases, ordinal positions (GROUP BY 1). HAVING clause. All aggregate functions. Multi-key grouping. Comprehensive GROUP BY support.

queue.task: Study sql-functions.js: built-in SQL functions
queue.summary: SQL functions: 716 lines, 70+ functions. String: UPPER/LOWER/TRIM/REPLACE/SUBSTR/CONCAT/POSITION/INITCAP/SPLIT_PART/OVERLAY/TRANSLATE/MD5. DateTime: EXTRACT/DATE_TRUNC/DATE_ADD. Numeric: ABS/ROUND/CEIL/FLOOR/POWER/SQRT/SIGN. Type: COALESCE/NULLIF/CAST. Comprehensive SQL function library.

queue.task: Study select-inner.js: inner select execution
queue.summary: select-inner.js: 686-line core SELECT orchestrator. Handles SELECT without FROM, GENERATE_SERIES, CTEs, table scanning, predicate pushdown, joins, subqueries, DISTINCT, ORDER BY, LIMIT/OFFSET, UNION/INTERSECT/EXCEPT. The heart of the query engine.

queue.task: Study data-loader.js: data pipeline
queue.summary: No data-loader module. Training loops use inline data handling. Gap for a proper DataLoader with batching/shuffling.

queue.task: Study file-wal.js: file-backed WAL
queue.summary: File WAL: 615 lines, 3 sync modes (immediate/batch/none). Group commit for throughput (PostgreSQL-style). Binary format, startup recovery, checkpoint tracking. Write buffer for batch mode. Production-grade WAL engineering.

queue.task: Git push
queue.summary: Git push done.

queue.task: Study pg-protocol.js: PostgreSQL protocol message handling
queue.summary: PG protocol: 513 lines, PostgreSQL wire protocol v3. Messages: AuthOK/MD5, ParameterStatus, BackendKeyData, ReadyForQuery, RowDescription, DataRow, CommandComplete, ErrorResponse. Type OID mapping. Full psql session support.

queue.task: Study event-system.js if exists: database event notifications
queue.summary: Event system: 510 lines total. LISTEN/NOTIFY (PostgreSQL pub/sub), event triggers (DDL hooks), DB events (query lifecycle), event emitter (28-line foundation). Full async notification system.

queue.task: Study avl-tree.js: balanced BST implementation
queue.summary: AVL tree: 92 lines, clean balanced BST. LL/RR/LR/RL rotations. Balance factor = height(left)-height(right), maintained within [-1,1]. Stricter than RB tree.

queue.task: Study vision.js if exists: vision model components
queue.summary: No ViT/vision modules. Conv2D and MaxPool2D exist for CNN image processing but no Vision Transformer. Gap.

queue.task: Study circular-log.js: circular buffer logging
queue.summary: Circular doubly-linked list: 25 lines. O(1) add/remove. Used by Clock replacer in buffer pool. Clean implementation with iterator support.

queue.task: Study connection-pool.js: connection management
queue.summary: Connection pool: 220 lines, production-quality. Async acquire with waiter queue, min/max size, health checks, idle timeout, max lifetime, acquire timeout, metrics. Used for PostgreSQL wire protocol server connections.

queue.task: Audit sparse-attention.js: sparse attention patterns
queue.summary: Sparse attention: 9 mask patterns (full, causal, local window, strided, dilated, global tokens, random, block-sparse, combined). BigBird-style and Longformer-style compositions. Sparse attention forward with mask application. Enables long-sequence processing.

queue.task: Git push
queue.summary: Git push done.

queue.task: Study column-store.js: columnar storage for OLAP
queue.summary: Column store: 228 lines. Per-column array storage, batch append, column-wise SUM/AVG/MIN/MAX, row reconstruction, projection. SIMD-friendly layout. Used by DuckDB/ClickHouse/Vertica. Good OLAP foundation.

queue.task: Study column-compression.js: columnar compression
queue.summary: Column compression: RLE + delta encoding + bit-packing at 123 lines. Same techniques as Apache Parquet/ORC. RLE for sorted, delta for timestamps, bit-packing for small integers. Correct implementations.

queue.task: Study cola.js: cache-oblivious lookahead array
queue.summary: COLA: 117 lines. Hierarchical sorted arrays, cascade merge when full. O(log²N/B) amortized inserts. Mathematically cleaner than LSM-trees. Academic data structure (Bender, Farach-Colton). Correct merge + lookup + range scan.

queue.task: Study clock-replacer.js: Clock page replacement
queue.summary: Clock replacer: 199 lines. PostgreSQL-style clock sweep with usage_count (0..5). Increment on access, decrement on sweep. Better than LRU for sequential flooding resistance. Heat-based eviction. Also implements LRU for comparison.

queue.task: Study batch-executor.js: batch DML operations
queue.summary: Batch executor: 114 lines. SQL script execution with statement parsing, transaction wrapping, error handling, progress callback.

queue.task: Study checksum.js: data integrity
queue.summary: Checksum: 18 lines. Adler-32, Fletcher-16, XOR. All correct. Adler-32 used in zlib, Fletcher-16 in TCP.

queue.task: Study aries-recovery.js: ARIES recovery algorithm
queue.summary: ARIES recovery: 479 lines implementing Mohan et al. 1992. Three-phase (Analysis->Redo->Undo). CLR records for idempotent undo. DPT + ATT for checkpoint. prevLsn chains for undo traversal. undoNextLsn in CLRs. THE foundational crash recovery algorithm. Production databases use this exact approach.

queue.task: Git push
queue.summary: Git push done. 200+ audits.

queue.task: Study advanced-ds.js: advanced data structures collection
queue.summary: Advanced DS: Fenwick Tree (BIT, i&-i trick correct), Segment Tree (configurable op, O(log n) range query), Union-Find (path compression + rank), Suffix Array (LCP), Double Hashing. Competitive programming foundations. All correct.

queue.task: Study bitwise-trie.js: bitwise prefix tree
queue.summary: Bitwise trie: 68 lines. HAMT-style with 5 bits/level, 32 children, 6 levels for 32-bit keys. O(7) lookup. Same approach as Clojure persistent vectors. Used for integer key indexes.

queue.task: Study btree-table.js: B-tree backed table
queue.summary: B+tree table: 265 lines. Clustered B+tree storage (SQLite/InnoDB style). Rows sorted by PK in leaf nodes. O(log n) point lookup, ordered range scan. HeapFile-compatible interface. THIRD storage layout in HenryDB (heap, columnar, clustered btree).

queue.task: Study connection-string.js: connection string parsing
queue.summary: Connection string parser: 164 lines. Parses postgres:// URLs and key=value DSN format. Extracts host, port, database, user, password, ssl, options. Environment variable fallbacks. PostgreSQL-compatible.

queue.task: Study catalog-queries.js: information_schema queries
queue.summary: Catalog queries: information_schema.tables, information_schema.columns, pg_catalog support. Makes database introspectable by tools (DBeaver, pgAdmin, psql). 296 lines.

queue.task: Study cli.js: command-line interface
queue.summary: CLI: psql-like REPL at 244 lines. Connects via PostgreSQL wire protocol. Supports -h host, -p port, -U user, -d dbname. Readline-based with history. Renders query results as formatted tables. Complete client for HenryDB server.

queue.task: Git push + final session update
queue.summary: Git push + final session update done. Architecture mapping complete.

queue.task: Study raft.js: Raft consensus algorithm for distributed DB
queue.summary: Raft consensus: 353 lines implementing Ongaro & Ousterhout 2014. Leader election (randomized timeouts), log replication (AppendEntries), commit rules (majority), safety (election restriction), heartbeats, state machine. Simulated networking. Core protocol is correct.

queue.task: Study lsm-tree.js: Log-Structured Merge tree
queue.summary: LSM tree: 893 lines total (tree + basic + compaction). SkipList memtable, SSTable sorted runs, TOMBSTONE deletes, multi-level with size ratios, leveled + tiered compaction. Foundation of RocksDB/LevelDB/Cassandra. Correct implementation.

queue.task: Study full-text-search.js: FTS capabilities
queue.summary: Full-text search: 374 lines, PostgreSQL-compatible! TSVector + TSQuery, simplified Porter stemmer, stop words, inverted index, TF-IDF ranking, phrase matching, weight-based ranking (A/B/C/D). Complete FTS engine.

queue.task: Study graph.js: graph database features
queue.summary: Graph: 120 lines. Adjacency list with BFS, DFS, Dijkstra, topological sort, cycle detection. Directed/undirected + weighted edges. Used internally for query plan optimization and dependency resolution.

queue.task: Study gossip.js: gossip protocol for cluster management
queue.summary: SWIM gossip: 225 lines implementing Das et al. 2002. Random ping, indirect probe, suspicion + incarnation numbers, piggyback dissemination, O(1) per member. Same protocol as HashiCorp Memberlist (Consul/Nomad). Simulated but algorithm-correct.

queue.task: Study skip-list.js: probabilistic data structure
queue.summary: Skip list: 235 lines, P=0.25 (Redis), MAX_LEVEL=32. Geometric level generation. O(log n) search/insert/delete. Range scan via level-0 links. Used as LSM memtable. Correct implementation per Pugh 1990.

queue.task: Study replication.js: database replication
queue.summary: Replication: 172 lines. Primary-Replica via statement-based SQL forwarding over LISTEN/NOTIFY channel. Sequence numbers for ordering. ReplicationPublisher hooks execute(), ReplicationSubscriber replays. PostgreSQL-inspired architecture.

queue.task: Git push
queue.summary: Git push done.

queue.task: Study jsonpath.js: JSONPath query support
queue.summary: JSONPath: 389 lines implementing RFC 9535 / PostgreSQL jsonpath. Supports $, .key, [index], [*], .*, .. (recursive descent), filter expressions. Tokenizer + compiler + evaluator. Complete JSON query engine.

queue.task: Study type-system.js: database type system
queue.summary: Type system: 36 lines. 6 types (INT, FLOAT, VARCHAR, BOOL, DATE, NULL). Type inference, coercion, compatibility checking. Simple but functional.

queue.task: Study zone-maps.js: zone maps for pruning
queue.summary: Zone maps: 163 lines. Per-page min/max tracking. Predicate-based page pruning (skip impossible pages). 1024 rows/page. Same as Parquet row group statistics / DuckDB zone maps. Supports =, <, >, <=, >=, BETWEEN predicates.

queue.task: Study schema-diff.js: schema comparison
queue.summary: Schema diff: 113 lines. Compares source/target schemas. Detects added/removed/modified tables and columns. Generates migration SQL (CREATE TABLE, ADD COLUMN, ALTER COLUMN). Like pg_diff/sqldiff.

queue.task: Study lock-manager.js: comprehensive lock management
queue.summary: Lock manager: 162 lines. 5 lock modes (IS, IX, S, SIX, X) with full 5x5 compatibility matrix. Deadlock detection via waits-for graph cycle detection! Lock upgrade, wait queue, per-tx tracking. Textbook 2PL implementation.

queue.task: Study migrations.js: schema migrations
queue.summary: Migrations: 198 lines. Versioned up/down migrations with _migrations tracking table. Transaction-wrapped, rollback support, status display. Like Alembic/Flyway/knex. Complete schema migration system.

queue.task: Git push
queue.summary: Git push done.

queue.task: Study consistent-hash.js: consistent hashing for distributed DB
queue.summary: Consistent hashing: 158 lines. Karger 1997. FNV-1a hash, 150 vnodes/physical node, sorted ring with binary search. AddNode/RemoveNode redistributes ~1/N keys. DynamoDB/Cassandra-style.

queue.task: Study prepared-statements.js: prepared statement support
queue.summary: Prepared statements: 179 lines. PREPARE/EXECUTE/DEALLOCATE with $1-style parameters. Plan caching, LRU eviction (max 100), type-safe binding, stats tracking. PostgreSQL-compatible.

queue.task: Study query-rewriter.js: SQL query rewriting optimizations
queue.summary: Query rewriter: 365 lines, 5 AST-to-AST transformation rules: view expansion, predicate pushdown, subquery flattening, constant folding, redundant predicate elimination. Rule-based query optimizer. Stats tracking per rule.

queue.task: Study streaming.js: streaming query results
queue.summary: Streaming: Count-Min Sketch (Cormode & Muthukrishnan 2005) at 145 lines. Probabilistic frequency counter. width×depth hash table. Query = min across all rows. Overestimates, never underestimates. Used in network monitoring, NLP, DB query optimization.

queue.task: Study cursor.js: database cursors
queue.summary: Cursor: 36 lines. Simple array-backed cursor with next/peek/current/fetch(n)/reset/close. Iterator protocol support. Not lazy streaming (materializes results first).

queue.task: Git push + update scratch findings
queue.summary: Git push done.

queue.task: Study two-phase-commit.js: distributed transaction coordination
queue.summary: 2PC: 191 lines. Coordinator + Participant pattern. Phase 1 (Prepare/Vote), Phase 2 (Commit/Abort). Crash simulation + recovery. WAL logging. Correctly notes the blocking problem (2PC Achilles heel). Used in XA transactions.

queue.task: Study interval-tree.js: interval data structure
queue.summary: Interval tree: 70 lines. Augmented BST with max-high tracking. Overlap query (point and range). O(log n + k) for k overlapping intervals. Used for temporal, scheduling, geospatial.

queue.task: Study hash-index.js: hash-based index
queue.summary: Hash index: 104 lines. Chained hashing, 0.75 load factor threshold, dynamic doubling. O(1) average equality lookup. FNV-1a hash. Standard implementation.

queue.task: Study two-phase-locking.js: 2PL protocol
queue.summary: 2PL: 140 lines. Growing/shrinking phase enforcement. S/X lock modes. Phase violation detection (cant acquire in shrinking). Guarantees serializability. Separate from the 5-mode lock manager — this is strict 2PL protocol.

queue.task: Study snapshot.js: snapshot management
queue.summary: Snapshot: 34 lines. Immutable frozen copy of Map data. Consistent-read semantics. SnapshotStore tracks history. Simple but correct.

queue.task: Study thread-pool.js: worker pool
queue.summary: Work-stealing thread pool: 213 lines. Per-worker deques (owner bottom, thief top), random victim selection, idle work stealing. Blumofe & Leiserson 1999. Same algorithm as Java ForkJoinPool/Intel TBB/Tokio. Simulated but algorithm-correct.
